summary: Quality-of-life summonses roughly doubled from FY22 to FY23 and kept climbing through FY25. NYPD attributes this to a rising volume of 311 quality-of-life complaints and, starting in FY25, the rollout of dedicated Quality of Life Teams (Q-Teams) tasked with addressing chronic quality-of-life conditions, alongside increases in specific categories like public urination and noise summonses.
